name: Way of St. James Loop from Saint-Palais
description: Cycle the difficult 19.5-mile Loop of Saint-Palais, gaining 1477 feet over 2 hours, enjoying coastal views and pine forests.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Saint-Palais traverse the diverse landscapes of the Basque Country, featuring a mix of foothills, mid-mountains, and river valleys. The region is characterized by undulating terrain, offering both challenging ascents and gentler riverside paths along the Bidouze River. Cyclists can explore areas with views of the distant Pyrénées Mountains and extensive countryside, including forests. This strategic location provides varied terrain suitable for different levels of touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Saint-Palais The most popular touring cycling route is Way of St. James Loop from Saint-Palais, a 19.5 miles (31.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects to the historic pilgrimage path, offering a unique cultural cycling experience. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bridge over the Saison River – Ostabat-Asme loop from Saint-Palais, a difficult 42.3 miles (68.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and scenic views across the Basque Countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Voie Verte – wonderful, quiet bike path loop from Saint-Palais, a 33.0 miles (53.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. This moderate route provides a quieter cycling experience. Touring cycling around Saint-Palais is defined by its foothills, river valleys, and connections to the Camino de Santiago. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate riverside paths to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Palais's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Saint-Palais?
A: Saint-Palais offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists, ranging from the undulating foothills and mid-mountains of the Basque Country to gentler river valleys. You'll find routes with challenging ascents and rewarding descents, as well as picturesque paths along the Bidouze River. The distant Pyrénées Mountains often provide a stunning backdrop, and the region transitions quickly from river valleys to varied Béarn landscapes.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Saint-Palais?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Saint-Palais, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored many of these, contributing to an average rating of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, Saint-Palais offers routes suitable for various abilities. While many routes feature significant elevation changes, there are also easier options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41603980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Voie Verte – wonderful, quiet bike path loop from Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that provides a quieter cycling experience through varied terrain. The paths along the Bidouze River also tend to be gentler and more accessible.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Saint-Palais is rich in history and natural beauty. You can encounter significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele&lt;/a&gt;, where the major French routes of the Camino de Santiago converge. Other points of interest include the historic village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ostabat&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3402827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Legend&lt;/a&gt;, and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/930773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapelle de Soyarza&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Iparlatze&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling routes that pass through Saint-Palais?
A: Absolutely. Saint-Palais is a significant stop on the pilgrimage routes to Santiago de Compostela, and it's also part of the major European cycling route, the Scandibérique / EuroVelo 3. This route provides dedicated infrastructure and signposting, connecting Saint-Palais to a wider network of cycling possibilities. You can find more information on the Scandibérique / EuroVelo 3 from Urt to Saint-Palais &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/the-scandiberique-eurovelo-3/urt-saint-palais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;here&lt;/a&gt; and from Saint-Palais to Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/the-scandiberique-eurovelo-3/saint-palais-saint-jean-pied-de-port" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;here&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Saint-Palais?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along riverside paths, though some of the more exposed routes might be warm. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Saint-Palais?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Saint-Palais are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ostabat – Col d'Iparlatze loop from Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride with significant elevation changes and scenic views.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Saint-Palais?
A: The touring cycling routes in Saint-Palais are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging climbs to serene river paths, and the rich cultural and historical context provided by its connection to the Camino de Santiago.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with exploring the cultural heritage of Saint-Palais?
A: Absolutely. Saint-Palais is steeped in history, particularly its role on the Camino de Santiago. Cyclists can take breaks to explore the Espace Chemins Bideak, which details the history of Lower Navarre and the pilgrimage. The town also features traditional Basque architecture, historic churches, and the Saint Jayme trinquet, the first Basque pelota court built in the Basque Country.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly scenic views of the Pyrenees?
A: While Saint-Palais is in the foothills, many routes offer glimpses of the majestic Pyrénées Mountains on the horizon. Routes that venture into the mid-mountains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41605145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Stele – Osquich Pass loop from Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt;, provide elevated vantage points for more expansive views of the surrounding landscapes and distant peaks.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available from Saint-Palais?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24647781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Saison River – Ostabat-Asme loop from Saint-Palais&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 68 kilometers (42 miles). This difficult path features significant elevation changes and offers extensive scenic views across the Basque Countryside.
tips: In the former Franciscan monastery there is now a modern tourist and pilgrim information center which also rents pilgrim rooms in the old monastery.
Near Ostabat, the Gibraltar Stele marks the spot where the three great routes to Santiago meet.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Loop of Saint-Palais?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for some segments that might include unpaved or temporary limestone surfaces. You'll encounter dedicated cycle paths, especially along the coast, but in more urbanized areas, cycle lanes can be on busy pavements, requiring you to be ready to move onto the road if traffic is heavy.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Loop of Saint-Palais for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This specific route is graded as difficult. While many local loops around Saint-Palais-sur-Mer are easy to moderate, this longer regional tour involves more varied relief and a significant elevation gain of 450 meters, making it more challenging than typical flat coastal rides. It requires good fitness and experience with touring cycling.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by several interesting spots. Keep an eye out for the Chemins-Bideak Pilgrims' and Tourist Information Center, Saint-Palais, which can be a good stop for local information. Another notable landmark is the Gibraltar Stele. The route also offers stunning sea views along the Atlantic coast, maritime pine forests, and glimpses of Belle Époque villas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route integrates with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Le Chemin de Saint-Jacques, Orthez - Estérençuby, and the EV3 France 49. It also briefly touches upon the Saint-Palais - Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port and Chemin de Xibaltarre / Xibaltarreko bidea.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Loop of Saint-Palais?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this exact loop isn't detailed, Saint-Palais-sur-Mer and surrounding towns offer various public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the starting point in Saint-Palais, possibly close to the Chemins-Bideak Pilgrims' and Tourist Information Center, which is a key highlight on the route.

faqs.question: Is the Loop of Saint-Palais dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The research does not specifically mention dog-friendly policies for this cycling route. Generally, dogs on a leash are often permitted on public cycle paths, but it's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead, especially concerning nature reserves or specific beach access points you might pass.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The region is popular for cycling from spring through autumn. The best time would likely be late spring (May-June) or early autumn (September-October) when the weather is pleasant, and the summer crowds have thinned. Summer can be very warm, and coastal areas might experience strong winds.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Loop of Saint-Palais?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Loop of Saint-Palais. The route utilizes public roads and cycle paths, including sections of the Vélodyssée, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect a rich variety of landscapes. The route offers stunning sea views along the Atlantic coast, particularly on sections following the 'Côte Sauvage'. You'll also pass through charming sandy beaches, maritime pine forests, and potentially glimpse elegant Belle Époque villas. Historical elements like blockhouses and legendary rocks add to the diverse scenery.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the Loop of Saint-Palais?
faqs.answer: Given that the route passes through Saint-Palais and potentially near other towns like Royan, you will find opportunities for cafes, pubs, and restaurants. The Chemins-Bideak Pilgrims' and Tourist Information Center in Saint-Palais could also provide information on local amenities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' grading and 31km distance, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your touring bicycle, and appropriate cycling gear. Sun protection is essential for coastal sections, and layers are recommended for changing weather. A map or GPS device is also advisable to navigate the various intersecting paths.

waypoints.name: Chemins-Bideak Pilgrims' and Tourist Information Center, Saint-Palais
